dict.string:
Shortcut Smith — an agentic shortcut that builds shortcuts FOR you.
HOW IT WORKS: 1. You describe what you want ("text my wife my ETA when I leave work"). 2. It sends that to a model and asks for a step-by-step shortcut RECIPE (in the awesome-shortcuts prompt format). 3. It shows you the recipe and saves it to a "Shortcut Recipes" note. 4. You build it in ~60 seconds — or hand the recipe to your agent, or submit it to awesome-shortcuts as a PR.
HONEST BOUNDARY: iOS has no action that silently creates and installs a brand-new shortcut from text — so Shortcut Smith produces the buildable RECIPE/blueprint, not a finished .shortcut. That's the same prompt format every entry in this repo ships, so the recipe IS the spec. (It also means no shortcut can conjure itself behind your back — by design.)
SETUP: for the reasoning step, use the on-device Apple Foundation Models via the Use Model action — AFM 3 Core (Foundation Models framework), no network and no key — or put a third-party model API endpoint + key in "Get Contents of URL". Add to Siri ("build me a shortcut") and the Share Sheet.
What should the shortcut do? Describe it in one sentence.
🛠️ Here's your shortcut recipe. Build it, or submit it to awesome-shortcuts.
🛠️ Recipe saved to your Shortcut Recipes note.
